title: Confirm a refund or reversal
preconditions: Both Canonical Transactions exist; the original is an outflow on an owned account; the owner or agent explicitly selected the proposed relationship.
postconditions: The active refund belongs in full to one original within the magnitude cap; incompatible roles are absent; current category and pool actuals offset exactly; all source and relationship history remains queryable.
main_flow: 1. The caller retrieves the original outflow, proposed credit, current category and spend pool, existing refunds, active roles, and supplies an idempotency identity. 2. The owner or caller explicitly requests a refund or reversal link. 3. Tally validates account, currency, opposite signs, lifecycle, role exclusivity, credit cardinality, and cumulative magnitude. 4. Tally records the attributable relationship without changing either source fact. 5. The credit reduces External Spend in its own Effective Date period and follows the original current category and spend pool. 6. Tally returns the relationship and exact pool/category effect.
alt_flows: 3a. Account or currency differs, signs do not oppose, a participant is inactive, or cumulative refund exceeds original -> reject
3b. Either participant has an incompatible transfer or refund role -> return a stable conflict
4a. Relationship write fails -> leave facts and prior relationships unchanged
5a. Original category or pool is corrected later -> apply linked offset to the new current attribution
5b. Refund Effective Date is later -> subtract in that later period even when original is outside it
metadata: {"depth_tier":3,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"A credit appears to refund or reverse some or all of an earlier spend transaction.","status":"draft"}
